feedback.text: I fully agree with the Digital Fairness Act. Taking just the example of a game such as Counter Strike 2 and its loot boxes, which are in fact matured and open casinos for all ages, sees it as a problem to be taken seriously for consumer safety in the long term, but I would add one additional point to this initiative: it would concern misleading commercial practices around the ownership and preservation of certain video games: Over the last 10 years, I have lost 7 different video games on which I actively played. And these games were never sold by them as “perishable” goods. Some people even cost money when they leave! While today, the only thing these games provide are empty screens with a message “impossible to connect to the server”. I find this unacceptable, knowing that the video game is considered to be the 10th art in our time, and does not deserve to be vulgently thrown into the bin! That is why I believe that adding a point around the current destruction of video games deserves a lot to be included in the Digital Fairness Act. Thank you for reading!
